name: Red Peppers Stuffed with Shrimp
recipeIngredient: 1 can Water chestnuts, drained and thinly sliced (8 oz.)
Salt and freshly ground white pepper
1/2 cup Monterey Jack cheese, finely shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Halve the peppers lengthwise and remove seeds and membranes.
3. Blanch in boiling water for 4 minutes, then drain.
4. Melt butter and sauté onion until soft, do not brown.
5. Sprinkle with flour and cook until bubbly.
6. Remove from heat and blend in sour cream. Cook over low heat until thickened.
7. Add lemon juice, mustard, parsley, water chestnuts, and shrimp. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Spoon into pepper shells and sprinkle tops with cheese.
9. Bake 30 minutes until heated through and cheese is bubbly.
